CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-23-2016 Continued To Jul-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Sargent of Computerlaw Group LLP telephone number 650-327-9800 for Plaintiff Marc Canter.
Nicholas Costanza and Richard Nelson of Sideman & Bancroft LLP telephone number 415-392-1960 for Defendants Cola, Inc., David Temkin and Michael McEvoy.
Jason Brown of Fisher & Phillips LLP telephone number 415-490-9018 for Defendants Cola, Inc., David Temkin and Michael McEvoy.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling with additional language. DEFENDANTS DAVID TEMKIN, MICHAEL MCEVOY and COLA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth, seventh, eighth, ninth, tenth, thirteenth and fourteenth causes of action and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action. The fourth, fifth, seventh, eighth, ninth, thirteenth and fourteenth causes of action are all based on the existence of a partnership among Mr. Canter, Mr. Temkin and Mr. McEvoy involving the same business matters as Cola, Inc. While there is no California case directly on point, California case law in similar contexts and case law from other jurisdictions establish that where, as here, a corporation predates an alleged partnership involving the same business matters as the corporation and the corporation involves creditors and investors different from those involved in the partnership, the partnership cannot co-exist with the corporation and thus the partnership does not exist as a matter of law and the purported partners do not owe any partnership duties to each other. The tenth cause of action fails because Cola, Inc. does not owe any fiduciary duties to Mr. Canter as a matter of law. The sixth cause of action is uncertain because it is based on legally insupportable partnership fiduciary duties and legally supportable majority shareholder fiduciary duties. Mr. Canter is granted leave to amend to delete the allegation of partnership fiduciary duties from the sixth cause of action. Leave to amend is not limited to the 6th cause of action, but can cover any additional claims/alegations with respect to the corporations existence rather than the partnership. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that includes the additional language.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Mar-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JACK RUSSO, ESQ. of Computerlaw Group LLP, Tel No. 650-327-9800 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JASON P. BROWN, ESQ. of Fisher & Phillips LLP, Tel No. 415-490-9000 appearing for Defendants David Temkin, Michael McEvoy, and Cola, Inc.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the modification to deny motion without prejudice, and orders as follows: As to DEFENDANTS DAVID TEMKIN, MICHAEL MCEVOY AND COLA, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings, Defendants' motion to compel arbitration is denied in its entirety. Although Mr. Canter alleges claims relating to his employment by Cola and his rights as a stockholder of Cola, it is clear that the gravamen of this action is his allegation that Mr. Temkin and Mr. McEvoy unlawfully used their majority control of Cola to push Mr. Canter out of the company and cause other harm to him. There is no arbitration agreement covering claims between and among Mr. Canter, Mr. Temkin and Mr. McEvoy as to these issues. The arbitration provisions of the Trinet terms and conditions agreement only apply to disputes relating to Mr. Canter's employment with Trinet and Mr. Canter has not made any claims relating to his employment with Trinet. While the first, second and third claims for alleged unlawful actions relating to Mr. Canter's employment with Cola are likely covered by the arbitration provisions of the employment agreement (at least as to Cola) and the twelfth and thirteenth causes of action relating to Mr. Canter's rights as a shareholder are likely covered by the arbitration provisions of the restricted stock purchase agreement, this is an appropriate case not to enforce those provisions per the court's discretion under CCP 1281.2(c). There is a substantial risk that if some of Mr. Canter's claims were arbitrated, there would be conflicting rulings on issues of law or fact with the court adjudication of the non-arbitrable claims. Moreover, judicial economy strongly favors deciding all of Mr. Canter's claims, which arise out of the same set of common facts, in a single forum, and the only forum that can do so is this court because of the lack of any arbitration agreement covering Mr. Temkin and Mr. McEvoy. The motion is denied without prejudice to Defendants re-filing the motion should the procedural circumstances in the case change. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
